title: AEGIS Dispute Evidence
description: Structured evidence format for AEGIS Protocol dispute submissions. This document is JSON-stringified and hex-encoded before on-chain submission.

skillHash.description: The bytes32 skill hash being disputed

attestationIndex.description: Zero-based index of the attestation being disputed

claimedLevel.description: The audit level the attestation claims (L1/L2/L3)

findings.description: One or more findings that demonstrate the attestation is invalid

criteriaId.description: The criteria ID that was incorrectly assessed

claimedPass.description: What the auditor claimed (typically true, since attestations require all criteria to pass)

actualResult.description: The challenger's assessment: 'fail' = criteria actually fails, 'not_tested' = auditor did not test this, 'insufficient_evidence' = auditor's metadata lacks evidence for this claim

description.description: Detailed description of the finding with specific evidence

reproduction.description: Step-by-step reproduction instructions to verify the finding

evidenceHash.description: SHA-256 hash of supporting evidence file (logs, screenshots, tool output)

metadataAnalysis.description: Analysis of the auditor's published metadata document

metadataURI.description: The metadata URI from the on-chain attestation

metadataAccessible.description: Whether the metadata was retrievable at the time of dispute

validationErrors.description: Validation errors from validateAuditMetadata(), if any

notes.description: Additional observations about the metadata quality

conclusion.description: Summary of why the attestation should be considered invalid

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this evidence was compiled
